entry.title: Washington Post
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Russia-Ukraine war live updates: Death toll in apartment attack expected to rise; Zelensky vows punishment
  • Shinzo Abe’s family to hold private wake for slain former Japan leader
  • Ukraine criticizes Canada’s move to return Russian gas turbine to Germany

  • Strike on apartment complex in eastern Ukraine kills 15, traps more under rubble

  • Gunmen kill 15, wound 9 in South Africa’s Soweto township

  • Spain: Three runners gored in tense 5th Pamplona bull run
  • Mexico, US presidents to meet amid newly tense relationship

  • Yosemite wildfire doubles in size, threatens over 500 giant sequoias
  • Pregnant woman given HOV ticket argues fetus is passenger, post-Roe

  • Takeaways from the Uber Files investigation
  • Response from Travis Kalanick to the Uber Files
  • Uber leveraged violent attacks against its drivers to pressure politicians
  • ‘Hit the kill switch’: Uber used covert tech to thwart government raids

  • Twitter Wants You to Build a Bot. So I Did
  • Boris Johnson’s Fall Is Populism’s Latest Act of Self-Destruction
  • How Sri Lanka Landed in a Political and Economic Crisis and What It Means
  • Why US Sees Saudi Arabia Again as Partner, Not Pariah
  • Why Banks Face Billions in ‘Hung Debt’ as Deals Cool
  • The reordering power of a recession may come as a shock to younger workers
  • The Post endorses Trone and Parrott in the Maryland 6th District primaries
  • Twitter’s case against India is crucial to the internet’s future
  • Biden has put the forced-birth crusaders on notice
  • Tax land, not buildings, to help with the housing crisis
  • A way around digital privacy concerns for women
  • A prayer for the dead
  • A Youngkin presidency would not change much
  • Ask Eugene Robinson about the latest news around the U.S. and world
  • Chat with Perry Bacon Jr. about politics and beyond
  • Chat with Jennifer Rubin about her columns, politics, policy and more
  • Need a dose of humor in your day? Chat with Alexandra Petri.
  • Send a letter to the editor
  • Contact the reader representative
  • Submit an op-ed
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: Sydney Morning Herald: 11 Jul, 2022

entry.content:

entry.title: Asahi Shimbun: 11 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Online exhibition focuses on work to restore Shuri-jo, Notre-Dame
  • ‘You’re next.’ LDP candidate threatened after attack on Abe
  • Handmade guns, targets found in home, vehicle of suspected killer
  • Police: Gunman followed Abe day before fatal shooting in Nara
  • Abe’s suspected killer led life of ‘hard times’ because of group
  • Nara police chief seeks to clarify undeniable flaws in Abe’s security
  • Festival dolls by former Japanese soldier displayed to honor him
  • Osaka students ask parties about improving lives of single moms
  • Nippon Ishin fails in Tokyo, Kyoto in bid to be a national party
  • A big win for bloc pushing to make Abe’s dream of revision a reality
  • LDP execs vow to work to fulfill political legacy of fallen Abe
  • Despite gains, mood somber in successful LDP candidate offices
  • Voters cast ballots in higher numbers in poll after Abe slaying
  • Ruling coalition on course for a big win in Upper House poll
  • Top US diplomat to pay Abe condolence visit to Japan
  • Chinese bank depositors face police in angry protest
  • Myanmar’s junta rolls out Chinese camera surveillance systems in more cities--sources
  • Blinken hits at ASEAN for lack of pressure on Myanmar
  • US eyes counter-China moves in Southeast Asia
  • Sri Lanka president, PM to resign after tumultuous protests
  • Silk painting of tragic warlord to go on show after full restoration
  • VOX POPULI: Century-old critique of upper chamber still rings true today
The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 11 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 11 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-11 06:03AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Despite presenting many challenges, pandemic-era remote learning also helped parents better understand their child’s ability and focus, as well as gain more appreciation for the work of teachers
  • As fuel prices shoot up, Hong Kong taxi drivers work smarter, other motorists choose to leave the car at home
  • Hong Kong housing chief vows to locate more land for transitional homes after visit to subdivided flats and ‘cage homes’
  • Batches of Haagen-Dazs vanilla ice cream pulled from Hong Kong shelves for containing banned pesticide
  • Hong Kong residents crossing border at Shenzhen Bay Port can soon book Covid-19 tests online as eager travellers throng checkpoint
  • Hong Kong Science Museum’s dinosaur exhibition: what you need to know, from the ‘Big Eight’ skeletons to touching fossils and baby Toni
  • Mutual trust is key to success of ‘one country, two systems’ after President Xi Jinping’s promise to keep Hong Kong’s governing policy unchanged
  • China’s car, internet and property sectors to rebound in second half on back of regulatory easing, stimulus measures, Credit Suisse says
  • Should Nato be worried about China and Russia’s influence in Africa?
  • White-shirted group attacks protesters at China banking scandal demonstration
  • Chinese workers ditch cramped cubicles for remote locales, freedom – and just look at that view
  • Climate change, nuclear catastrophe, out-of-control AI: how US-China tech rivalry puts humanity at risk
  • Visit by China’s top diplomat underscores importance of Zimbabwe ties
  • China trade, spending rebound dulled by zero-Covid uncertainty
  • Relocation of UN’s COP15 biodiversity conference raises questions about its legacy for China
  • Xi Jinping and Joe Biden expected to talk within weeks: Antony Blinken
  • Head of China’s top court orders crackdown on ‘abhorrent’ crimes amid public outcry over attack on women in Tangshan
  • Shanghai to ramp up support for metaverse development, low-carbon projects, smart gadgets to help China’s economic recovery
  • China’s wind farms have a role in its renewable energy future, can they also ease effects of dust storms?
  • In Taiwan, Shinzo Abe’s death means loss of steadfast supporter
  • Pro-Beijing Kiribati quits Pacific Islands Forum as US-China tensions overshadow summit
  • Viral road rage outburst on Malaysia-Singapore bridge sparks police investigation
  • Chinese-built surveillance systems spread across junta-ruled Myanmar, including hundreds of Huawei cameras: sources
  • US woman’s TikTok on ‘third world’ Malaysia stirs debate about prejudice, misconceptions
  • Antony Blinken makes unexpected stop in Tokyo to pay respects after Shinzo Abe assassination
  • Japan’s ruling LDP wins big in upper house election after former PM Abe’s death
  • Sri Lanka protesters refuse to leave president, PM residences until the men quit
  • How Indian royals from Rajasthan to Orissa are helping revive traditional arts and crafts
  • US weighs possible resumption of offensive arms sales to Saudi Arabia
  • Nasa to reveal James Webb Space Telescope’s first full-colour, ‘deepest images of the Universe’
  • Uber lobbied politicians, broke laws in global push: investigation
  • French opposition denounces reports of ‘secret deal’ between Uber and Emmanuel Macron
  • UK foreign minister Liz Truss joins 11-strong race to replace Boris Johnson
  • Ghana fears Nigeria jail break jihadists may try to enter country
  • Ukraine ‘deeply disappointed’ after Canada exempts Russian gas turbine from sanctions
  • Israeli ministers Gideon Saar and Benny Gantz to run as partners in upcoming elections
  • Novak Djokovic sees off Nick Kyrgios to win seventh Wimbledon title and 21st grand slam
  • Faroe Islands limits killing of dolphins to 500 a year after anger over annual hunt
  • South Africa gunmen kill 19 people ‘randomly’ in two bar shootings, say police
  • Russian rockets kill 15 in block of flats in Ukraine’s Donbas, say officials
  • Demand for Singapore rental property is surging amid supply shortage and expat arrivals
  • Hong Kong stocks see sharpest drop in two weeks amid Shanghai, Macau Covid-19 flare-ups, fines on Alibaba, Tencent, Bilibili
  • Swap Connect with Hong Kong will help China attract more international investors to its bond markets, Standard Chartered banker says
  • Green stocks such as BYD and CATL are red hot again, as Chinese stocks flirt with bull market
  • As nuclear power makes a comeback, China is poised to be the net-zero hero
  • Macau to shut casinos for first time in two years as China’s gambling hub moves to curb rising number of new Covid-19 cases
  • Smallest flats see brisk demand at first Hong Kong home sale after Xi speech underlining desire for larger homes
  • To bridge divide at heart of US-China tensions, start with global rethink of public sector’s role
  • Why school leadership is a driving force for innovations in education
  • Ballad of a White Cow movie review: Maryam Moghadam’s haunting Iranian drama about a widow battling injustice
  • Singapore arts pioneer Tan Boon Hui’s death ‘a tremendous blow’ to Asian art
  • What is ‘face blindness’ and why does Brad Pitt say people hate him because of it?
  • Abortion in ancient China with TCM and acupuncture was unreliable – most women resorted to physically injuring themselves, as in one harrowing story
  • Why most overseas visitors to Japan are on business trips, with the country opening up cautiously
  • 5 Bali wellness experiences, from light therapy to breathwork, put to the test
  • Shenzhen in China becomes first mainland city to make ‘living wills’ into law covering end-of-life care
  • Alopecia areata, Jada Pinkett Smith’s hair-loss condition, now treatable by arthritis and Covid-19 drug, heralding ‘a new era’ for sufferers
  • Are Scientology founder L. Ron Hubbard’s far-fetched tales in China and Asia diaries actually true - at least partly?
  • How Olivier Rousteing paid homage to Jean Paul Gaultier: the Balmain designer’s one-off haute couture collection for Paris Fashion Week was tongue-in-cheek and full of drama
  • Meet Sonoya Mizuno, star of the Game of Thrones prequel: she danced alongside Emma Stone in La La Land and modelled for an Adidas campaign, but most will know her from Crazy Rich Asians
  • Meet Alastair Wallace Stewart, Rod Stewart’s dashing son: he’s following in mum Penny Lancaster’s footsteps, modelling for Italian fashion brand Palm Angels – but he also loves swimming and football
  • Saint Seb! 5 times Sebastian Vettel proved he’s the nicest guy in Formula One: from campaigning for LGBT rights and supporting Ukraine, to reforming motorsports to combat climate change
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.
